title: Can Democracy Endure Where the Cyber Things Are?
summary: "This article examines how executive power translates in cyberspace and assesses the president's ability to reconfigure U.S. network data flows to the global Internet during a national crisis."
body: Imagine if a U.S. president determined that an insurrection or a threat of war existed. Would President Lincoln's actions in blockading Confederate ports enable a modern president to suppress insurrection by " blockading " portions of U.S. Internet connections? Assuming the executive determined a denial-of-access necessary for national security purposes — perhaps to prevent domestic adversaries from relaying communications to fellow in-state conspirators or to impede their ability to communicate with an affiliated foreign adversary — is such government action lawful?...
